notes: Source of truth for the 35 seeded findings. Sync from Notion back to this file as Rob adds/edits entries during real audits. Variables in {ALL_CAPS} are per-customer substitutions.

variables.ACTUAL_NAME: Customer's product name from intake form
variables.PAGE: Specific page name where issue appears
variables.LIST: Array of items (broken links, errors, etc.)
variables.LIST_WITH_SIZES: List of oversized images with sizes
variables.LIST_WITH_STATUS_CODES: Failed network requests with status codes
variables.LIST_OF_ELEMENTS: Tap targets that are too small
variables.EXAMPLE: Specific instance of cross-user data leakage
variables.SPECIFIC_BEHAVIOR_NEEDED: What the form should do after submission

findings.name: Generic 'Your Company Name' left in headers/footers
findings.explanation: Your site still shows "Your Company Name" instead of your actual product name. This is one of the first things visitors notice — it signals "this isn't finished."

fix_prompts.lovable: On every page, find any instance of 'Your Company Name' or similar placeholder text in headers, footers, navigation, and metadata. Replace with the actual product name: {ACTUAL_NAME}. Don't change any code structure or styling — only the visible text.
fix_prompts.bolt: Search the entire codebase for placeholder text like 'Your Company Name' and replace with '{ACTUAL_NAME}'. Update headers, footers, page titles, and meta tags. Preserve all other code.
fix_prompts.generic: Find any instance of 'Your Company Name' or similar placeholder text and replace with the actual product name: {ACTUAL_NAME}. Check headers, footers, navigation, and metadata.

findings.name: Lorem ipsum still visible
findings.explanation: There's lorem ipsum placeholder text still showing on your site. Visitors will recognize it immediately.

fix_prompts.generic: Find any lorem ipsum or placeholder paragraph text on the site and replace it with real content. The content should be about: {ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}. Keep paragraphs roughly the same length as the placeholders.

findings.name: Default platform copy (Lovable starter text)

detection.description: Look for known Lovable starter phrases like 'Your AI-powered web application,' 'Welcome to your new app,' 'Get started by editing'

findings.explanation: Some of Lovable's default starter copy is still on your homepage. Visitors who've seen other Lovable apps will recognize it.

fix_prompts.lovable: Replace any default Lovable starter copy on the homepage with content specific to my app. My app is {ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}. Write a real hero headline, subheadline, and CTA. Keep the visual layout the same.

findings.notes: Build per-platform default-copy detection lists as patterns are observed.

findings.name: '[Insert X here]' brackets
findings.explanation: There are template instructions like "[Insert tagline here]" still visible on the page. These are meant to be replaced before launch.

fix_prompts.generic: Find any text in square brackets that looks like a template instruction (e.g., '[Insert tagline]', '[Your value prop]'). Replace each with actual content based on the surrounding context.

findings.name: TODO/FIXME comments rendered to users
findings.explanation: Internal developer notes ("TODO: add real description") are showing up on the live site as actual user-facing text.

fix_prompts.generic: Find any visible 'TODO' or 'FIXME' text on the live site and replace with the real content the note was asking for. Use the surrounding context to figure out what should be there.

findings.notes: Detect only visible text, not code comments. False positive risk: legitimate "TODO" in a real to-do app's UI.

findings.name: Placeholder email addresses
findings.explanation: Your contact/support email is still a placeholder like `support@example.com`. Visitors who try to reach you will bounce.

fix_prompts.generic: Find every instance of placeholder email addresses (anything @example.com, @yourdomain.com, @test.com) and replace with the real support email: {ACTUAL_EMAIL}. Check footer, contact page, mailto: links, and any 'help' or 'support' sections.

findings.name: 'Acme Inc' / 'Example Corp' default company
findings.explanation: References to "Acme Inc" or "Example Corp" are still showing up. These are default placeholder company names.

fix_prompts.generic: Replace any references to 'Acme', 'Example Corp', or similar placeholder company names with the actual product name: {ACTUAL_NAME}.

findings.notes: 'Acme' can be a legitimate brand name — verify before flagging.

findings.name: Missing /privacy page

detection.description: Try GET /privacy, /privacy-policy — returns 404

findings.explanation: Your site has no privacy policy page. Most users — and almost certainly the App Store, payment processors, and ad platforms — expect one.

fix_prompts.generic: Add a /privacy route with a basic privacy policy page. Include sections for: what data we collect, how we use it, how we share it (or don't), data retention, user rights, and contact info. Use {ACTUAL_EMAIL} for the contact section. Link to it from the footer.

findings.notes: Bump to Critical if the app collects any user data.

findings.name: Missing /terms page

detection.description: 404 on /terms, /terms-of-service, /tos

findings.explanation: Your site has no Terms of Service. Users have nothing to agree to, and payment processors usually require one.

fix_prompts.generic: Add a /terms route with basic Terms of Service. Include sections for: acceptance of terms, service description, user obligations, prohibited uses, termination, disclaimers, and contact info. Link from the footer.

findings.name: Missing /contact or contact info

detection.description: No visible contact email, no /contact page, no contact form

findings.explanation: Visitors can't easily find how to contact you. This hurts trust and conversion.

fix_prompts.generic: Add a visible way for users to contact me. Either add a /contact page with an email address ({ACTUAL_EMAIL}) and optional contact form, or put a clear support email in the footer of every page.

findings.name: Buttons that do nothing on click

detection.description: Click each visible button, observe DOM/navigation

findings.explanation: The "{BUTTON_NAME}" button doesn't do anything when clicked. Users will think the site is broken.

fix_prompts.generic: The {BUTTON_NAME} button on {PAGE} is not working — it appears clickable but does nothing when clicked. Investigate why and fix it so it performs its intended action: {EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR}. Don't change the button's appearance.

findings.name: Broken internal links (404s)

detection.description: Crawl all <a href> values, check status codes

findings.explanation: Some internal links lead to "page not found" errors. Specifically: {LIST_OF_BROKEN_LINKS}.

fix_prompts.generic: Fix these broken internal links: {LIST}. For each link, either point it to the correct page if it exists, or create the missing page, or remove the link entirely if no longer needed.

findings.name: Forms that don't submit

detection.description: Fill out form with test data, click submit, observe network tab and DOM

findings.explanation: The {FORM_NAME} form doesn't actually submit. Users fill it out, click submit, and nothing happens.

fix_prompts.generic: The {FORM_NAME} form on {PAGE} doesn't work — when users fill it out and click submit, nothing happens. Fix the form so submissions are saved or sent properly. {SPECIFIC_BEHAVIOR_NEEDED}.

findings.notes: Crawler must NEVER submit forms automatically. Manual-only detection.

findings.name: Forms submit with no confirmation

detection.description: Submit form, observe whether user receives any confirmation

findings.explanation: When users submit the {FORM_NAME} form, they don't get any confirmation that it worked. They'll wonder if it submitted at all.

fix_prompts.generic: After the {FORM_NAME} form submits, show a clear success message or redirect to a confirmation page. The user needs to know their submission worked.

findings.name: Console errors on page load

detection.description: Open DevTools, load page, capture all console errors

findings.explanation: Your browser shows {N} errors when the page loads. Some of these might be causing features to fail.

fix_prompts.generic: These JavaScript errors appear in the console when {PAGE} loads: {ERROR_LIST}. Investigate each and fix the underlying cause. Don't suppress errors — fix them.

findings.notes: Bump to High if functional features depend on the failing code.

findings.name: Failed network requests (4xx/5xx)

detection.description: Open Network tab, load page, filter for non-2xx responses

findings.explanation: When the page loads, some background requests are failing: {REQUEST_LIST}. Features depending on these requests will be broken.

fix_prompts.generic: These network requests are failing on {PAGE}: {LIST_WITH_STATUS_CODES}. Investigate each and fix the underlying issue — wrong URL, missing endpoint, auth failure, etc.

findings.name: Mobile layout overflow / horizontal scroll

detection.description: Open in 375px viewport, look for document.body.scrollWidth > window.innerWidth

findings.explanation: On mobile, your page scrolls sideways because something is wider than the screen. This makes the site feel broken on phones.

fix_prompts.generic: On mobile devices, {PAGE} has horizontal scrolling — something is wider than the viewport. Find what's overflowing (often a wide image, table, or container with fixed width) and make it responsive. Don't break the desktop layout.

findings.name: Text too small on mobile

detection.description: Body text rendering under ~14px on mobile

findings.explanation: Body text on mobile is too small to read comfortably (currently {SIZE}px). Most apps use at least 16px for body text on mobile.

fix_prompts.generic: On mobile, increase the body text to at least 16px. Currently it's rendering at {SIZE}px which is hard to read on phones.

findings.name: Tap targets too small

detection.description: Buttons/links smaller than 44×44px on mobile

findings.explanation: Some buttons are too small to tap easily on mobile (under 44×44 pixels). Apple and Google both recommend 44px as a minimum.

fix_prompts.generic: On mobile, increase tap target sizes for {LIST_OF_ELEMENTS} to at least 44×44 pixels. Either add padding or increase the button size directly.

findings.name: Logged-out access to protected pages

detection.description: Try GET /dashboard, /admin, /settings etc. without auth

findings.explanation: Users who aren't logged in can access {PROTECTED_ROUTES}. This may expose data they shouldn't see.

fix_prompts.lovable: These routes load content even when no user is signed in: {LIST}. Add authentication checks so that visiting these routes while logged out redirects to /login or shows an empty/error state. Use Supabase auth state to verify.
fix_prompts.bolt: Add auth guards to these routes: {LIST}. Verify the user has an active session before rendering content. Redirect unauthenticated users to /login.

findings.name: Cross-user data visible

detection.description: Sign in as User A, then as User B, compare what each can see

findings.explanation: User A can see User B's data (specifically: {EXAMPLE}). This means anyone who signs up can see other users' information.

fix_prompts.lovable: User A can see User B's {DATA_TYPE} on the {PAGE} page. This means Supabase queries aren't filtering by user. Add row-level security policies on the {TABLE_NAME} table that restrict reads to `auth.uid() = user_id`. Also update the query in the page component to filter by current user.

findings.notes: Cornerstone Critical finding. Requires two-account intake (Launch Pack+).

findings.name: Signup confirmation email never arrives

detection.description: Sign up with fresh email, wait 5+ minutes

findings.explanation: When new users sign up, the confirmation email never arrives. They can't complete signup.

fix_prompts.generic: When users sign up, they should receive a confirmation email but none arrives. Check the email provider configuration (Resend, SendGrid, or Supabase's built-in email), verify the sender domain is set up, and ensure the signup flow actually triggers the email send.

findings.name: Default meta description (platform default)

detection.description: Check <meta name="description"> against known platform defaults

findings.explanation: When your link is shared on social media, it shows the platform's default description instead of one about your actual app.

fix_prompts.generic: Update the meta description to describe my actual app: {ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}. Also set Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image) so the link previews well when shared on Twitter, LinkedIn, Slack, etc.

findings.name: No favicon (showing default platform icon)

detection.description: Check <link rel="icon"> — verify it's custom, not platform default

findings.explanation: Your site uses the default browser icon (or the platform's). It should have your own favicon so users recognize tabs.

fix_prompts.generic: Add a custom favicon. I'll provide an image (or generate a simple one based on the app name '{ACTUAL_NAME}'). Update the <link rel='icon'> tag and add the necessary icon files.

findings.name: Page title is generic ('My App' or platform default)

detection.description: Check <title> tag and per-page title overrides

findings.explanation: Your page titles in browser tabs are generic. Users won't see your app name in their tab list.

fix_prompts.generic: Update page titles so each page has a meaningful title with the app name. Format: 'Page Name | {ACTUAL_NAME}'. Update both the default <title> and any per-page overrides.

findings.name: No empty states (blank dashboards for new users)

detection.description: Sign in with brand-new account, observe dashboard/main pages

findings.explanation: When a new user signs in for the first time, the dashboard is completely empty with no instructions. They won't know what to do.

fix_prompts.generic: When a user has no {ITEMS} yet, the {PAGE} page shows nothing. Add a friendly empty state with: an icon or illustration, a short message ('No {ITEMS} yet'), and a clear CTA button to create the first one.

findings.name: No loading states

detection.description: Slow network throttle in DevTools, observe page during data fetch

findings.explanation: While data is loading, the page shows nothing — users see a blank screen and may think it's broken.

fix_prompts.generic: Add loading states (skeleton screens, spinners, or 'Loading...' text) to {LIST_OF_PAGES} so users see something is happening while data loads.

findings.name: Error states swallow errors silently

detection.description: Force errors (disconnect network, submit invalid data) and observe UI

findings.explanation: When something goes wrong, users get no feedback — the action just silently fails.

fix_prompts.generic: When operations fail (network errors, validation errors, server errors), show users a clear error message instead of failing silently. Use a toast notification or inline error text. Be specific about what went wrong when possible.

findings.name: No 'back' or navigation context

detection.description: Navigate deep into the app, observe whether back/breadcrumb is clear

findings.explanation: When users navigate deep into the app, there's no clear way back to where they came from.

fix_prompts.generic: Add either a back button or breadcrumb navigation on deeper pages so users can navigate back to where they were.

findings.name: Massive unoptimized images

detection.description: Check Network tab for >1MB images

findings.explanation: Some images on your site are very large (e.g., {SIZE}MB) and slow down page loads.

fix_prompts.generic: These images are oversized: {LIST_WITH_SIZES}. Compress them and use modern formats (WebP). Aim for under 200KB per image for hero/featured images and under 50KB for thumbnails.

findings.name: Render-blocking resources

detection.description: Lighthouse / WebPageTest

findings.explanation: Some scripts and stylesheets are blocking the page from rendering quickly. Users see a blank page for longer than necessary.

fix_prompts.generic: Defer non-critical JavaScript with the `defer` or `async` attributes. Inline critical CSS for above-the-fold content. The goal is for users to see content within 1.5 seconds on a typical connection.

findings.name: No 404 page

detection.description: Visit /nonexistent-route-12345

findings.explanation: When users hit a URL that doesn't exist, they get a generic browser error instead of a friendly 404 page.

fix_prompts.generic: Add a custom 404 page that shows when users visit URLs that don't exist. Include: a clear 'Page not found' message, a link back to home, and optionally a search or sitemap. Style it consistently with the rest of the site.

findings.name: Inconsistent terminology (customer/client/user)

detection.description: Manual scan for multiple terms referring to the same entity

findings.explanation: Your app uses "customer," "client," and "user" interchangeably to refer to the same thing. Pick one and use it consistently — it makes the app feel more thought-through.

fix_prompts.generic: The app uses 'customer,' 'client,' and 'user' to refer to the same role. Pick {PREFERRED_TERM} and replace the others everywhere in the UI. Don't change database table or column names — only user-facing labels.

findings.notes: This is the light-touch preview of the v1 Brand Tokens feature. Lead with it when offering an upsell.

findings.name: Capitalization inconsistency on UI labels

detection.description: Manual scan of buttons, headers, nav items

findings.explanation: Buttons and labels use inconsistent capitalization — some use Title Case, some use sentence case, some are ALL CAPS.

fix_prompts.generic: Standardize capitalization across all UI elements. Use sentence case for buttons ('Save changes', not 'Save Changes' or 'SAVE CHANGES') and title case for page headings. Apply this consistently throughout the app.

findings.name: Default Lovable / Bolt / Base44 branding still showing

detection.description: Search for 'Built with Lovable' or platform branding

findings.explanation: Your site still shows "Built with Lovable" or similar platform branding. If you're on a paid plan, you can remove this.

fix_prompts.lovable: Remove the 'Built with Lovable' badge from the site. This is in the project settings under branding. (Note: requires a paid Lovable plan.)

findings.notes: Bolt/Base44 equivalents to be added when first observed.

findings.name: Development tools visible on production URL

detection.description: Visible dev bypass, seed data buttons, 'Developer Tools' sections, or 'for development purposes only' on published URL

findings.explanation: Your live site still shows development-only features — like a login bypass, sample data seeders, or internal admin tools. Anyone who visits will see them.

fix_prompts.lovable: Remove all development-only UI from the published site: Dev Bypass buttons, 'Seed Sample Data', 'Developer Tools' sections, and any 'for development purposes only' text. Wrap these components in a check so they only render when import.meta.env.DEV is true (local dev only), never on the lovable.app published URL.
fix_prompts.generic: Hide or remove all development and testing UI from the production deployment. Use environment-based conditionals so dev tools never render on the public URL.

findings.name: Ratings displayed with zero reviews

detection.description: Star rating shown alongside (0) reviews or zero review count

findings.explanation: Listings show a star rating (e.g. 4.5) but zero reviews. That combination reads as fake social proof.

fix_prompts.generic: When review count is 0, do not show a numeric star rating. Show 'New' or 'No reviews yet' instead. Only display average rating when at least 1 review exists. Apply on all cards and detail pages.

findings.notes: Common on marketplace/demo listings with seeded data.

findings.name: Primary CTA opens flow that cannot complete

detection.description: Main CTA (Book, Quick Book, Get Started) opens modal/page that dead-ends — disabled submit, empty slots, no explanation

findings.explanation: Your main action button ("{BUTTON_NAME}") opens a flow that cannot be completed — for example, no time slots, a disabled submit button, or no message explaining why.

fix_prompts.generic: The {BUTTON_NAME} flow on {PAGE} dead-ends: {SPECIFIC_BEHAVIOR_NEEDED}. Either wire up the missing backend/availability so users can complete the action, or hide/disable the CTA on items that are not bookable yet and show a clear message like 'No dates available — check back soon'.

findings.notes: First observed: LiLo Quick Book with 'No available slots found'.
